method_atom: An adiabatic interpolation that slowly tunes a control parameter to steer a system from an easily-prepared initial configuration to the configuration encoding a target solution, where the cost of staying in the instantaneous lowest state is set by the smallest spectral gap between the two lowest energy levels along the path. The size-scaling of this minimum gap is characterized statistically as a median over a random ensemble of instances using stochastic Monte Carlo sampling. The bottleneck is a single localized two-level avoided crossing (Landau-Zener collision between the two lowest levels).
object_atom: The quantum adiabatic algorithm applied to the Exact Cover satisfiability (constraint-satisfaction) problem for sizes N <= 128, whose median complexity is benchmarked against the classical Davis-Putnam algorithm.
brief_reason: The adiabatic parameter-sweep, minimum-gap-scaling, and localized avoided-crossing machinery plus its median-over-random-instances Monte Carlo estimation transfer to any parameter-driven optimization, while the Exact Cover satisfiability instances and the quantum-vs-Davis-Putnam complexity comparison are the computer-science-specific object.
